transcript: Hi! My name’s Helmut with ASCAP. How are we today?
Good! I know you are under new management, so I thought I’d give you a call and introduce myself; I’m your new account rep. here at ASCAP.
No, I’m the old manager; you’re calling the wrong person.
Yeah, well I’m new to the account, so, ah—
—Well I’m—I just leased the place; I have nothing to do with Northern D’Lites anymore.
Well you got live music up there, don’t ya?
I don’t have anything. Let me spell it out to you again: I do not own the place anymore; I sold it last week. Bye—
—Do you guys got chickens on the sidewalk up ‘ere?
Uh, sir, it’s Helmut Frick with ASCAP. I just had a couple quick questions for you today, okay?
We just would like to get a small deposit just so that—
—I am not the owner! Do you understand? Can you understand me? I s—w—leased the place as of the 19th of last week; I do not have anything to do with Northern D’Lites.
—you don’t get behind, okay? If we could just get a $200 deposit we’ll be all set, okay, on your account here, today. Okay?
Listen, fuckface: I don’t owe you anything ‘cause I am not the owner! Do you understand English?
That’s not how it works, sir; this is ASCAP calling, okay?
Yes, Mario, this is ASCAP calling, okay? Now—
—Yeah, I gathered that, now you listen to me: I don’t have anything to do with Northern D’Lites anymore—
You’ve said to me you know it’s under new management. Why are you bothering me?
Well, before last week, you were playing the song by Don Comovilla called, “Taco Comovilla, Taco Comovilla!”—
—How ‘bout playing your mother’s pussy ya fuckin’ asshole, ya four-eyed fuck?!—
[unintelligible] I’m gonna come up there and fuckin’ gut ya, you motherfucker!—
[In background] Oh, for Christ sake! Why don’t you stop?!
[In background] [laughing] I don’t even know what—
Hello? Sir, when are you going to pay us for “Taco Comovilla, Taco Comovilla!” the song that you’ve been playing?
How ‘bout Como my ass again. Listen to me: I don’t own it anymore!; I don’t owe you any money! As a matter of fact, you owe me money, so bust off and go fuck yourself!
Yes, sir. We’re just gonna send a rep. out to pick a check up, okay? This evening, alright?
$200 and you’re done for playing one song!
Yeah: “Taco Comovilla, Taco Comovilla!” This is ASCAP calling—
It means, “stick it up your ass, ya asshole!”
Sir, this is Helmut Frick with ASCAP. What do we need to do to get through with ya here, today?

How ‘bout listening? Take the fuckin’ shit out of your ears and listen to what I’m saying: I do not own it anymore; I’ve been closed for fucking the last year, douchebag! How ‘bout that? Can you understand that now?!
I wanna come over and pick a check up, is what I need from ya here today!—
—Yeah, come on over, then! No problem! I’ll be here! [exhales]
You played the song by Don Comovilla; that’s $200 price tag associated with that song, okay? [sfx]
Yeah, no problem! I—I—Matter of fact, I played it twice that evening! I’ll pay you $400! How’s that?
I’ll send a rep. over and I want you—
—I want you to be serious when he shows up—

—And meanwhile, why don’t you take some fuckin’ toilet paper? Because I’m gonna drive down there after you pick the check up and I’m gonna pull your asshole up to your fuckin’ ears so I don’t have to fuck—you can’t hear me anymore, ya fuck!
